purpose: This repository contains data transformation tools for CrossRiver Bank's lending and selling operations. It uses DBT (Data Build Tool) to transform data from source systems into a data warehouse, with Apache Airflow orchestrating the ETL pipelines in both local development and cloud (AWS) environments.
businessFeatures: Reference data management for loan types, statuses, and entities

apis.purpose: Ingests data from source systems into the data warehouse

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core entity representing loans being processed in the system

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Represents loan contracts with associated terms and conditions

dataEntities.name: LoanSaleStatusEntity
dataEntities.description: Reference data for loan sale statuses

dataEntities.name: SeasoningPeriodEntity
dataEntities.description: Reference data for loan seasoning periods

dataEntities.name: Fee
dataEntities.description: Fee structures and parameters applied to loans

dataEntities.name: VolumeFee
dataEntities.description: Volume-based fee calculations for loan portfolios

messagingPatterns.name: ECSTaskExecution
messagingPatterns.description: Orchestrates ETL tasks execution via AWS ECS Fargate

findings.title: Hardcoded credentials in aws_utils.py
findings.description: The file dags/projects/utils/aws_utils.py contains hardcoded S3 credentials (username and password) for local development. While these appear to be for local development only, hardcoded credentials are a security risk and should be moved to environment variables or a dedicated configuration file that is not tracked in source control.

findings.title: Incomplete bootstrapping DAG implementation
findings.description: The bootstrapping DAG file (dags/projects/cos_lending_selling/bootstrapping_dag.py) contains TODO comments indicating unfinished implementation. This could cause problems when deploying to production as the ETL pipeline may not function correctly or may be missing critical components.

findings.title: Duplicate source definitions across DBT projects
findings.description: Multiple DBT projects (export, loans_curation, seasoning) define the same source tables independently. This creates maintenance overhead and risk of inconsistency when source schemas change. Consider centralizing source definitions in a shared package.
